results.possibleIdentity: Pascal B. Greene was a computer specialist and veteran who worked for Hewlett-Packard (HP) in Paramus, New Jersey, during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded to facilitate collaborations between artists, engineers, and scientists. Greene's professional profile, as recorded in the E.A.T. technical services index, identifies him as an expert in computer technology. Earlier in his life, Greene served in the United States Army during the Korean War as a member of the 19th Infantry Regiment, 24th Infantry Division. Military records indicate he was from New York and was evacuated from the conflict in April 1951. Following his service, he resided in Fort Smith, Arkansas, where he was a licensed amateur radio operator with the call sign W5BZZ as of 1954.
